title: centella chamissonis
intro: Centella chamissonis, also known as Pacific or Western marshwort, is a type of perennial herb native to the western coast of North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Centella chamissonis include improving cognitive function, reducing inflammation, and promoting wound healing, making it a popular ingredient in natural remedies for skin and brain health. Centella chamissonis can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, capsules, and topical creams, allowing for flexible application and administration.
